chunk_summary: The checklist for school-based nutrition and health interventions under PM POSHAN ensures effective implementation of meals, IFA tablets, deworming, and fortified staples, addressing pediatric anemia, growth, and infection risks.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of PM POSHAN in delivering daily meals and preventive health interventions in schools.
Learn how to verify workflows for IFA tablets, deworming, and fortified staples in school settings.
Identify strategies to address non-adherence and acceptability issues in pediatric nutrition programs.
key_facts: PM POSHAN provides daily hot cooked meals to schoolchildren as part of India's nutrition programs.
Weekly IFA tablets must be age-appropriate, supervised during administration, and monitored for side effects.
Biannual National Deworming Day excludes acutely unwell children and requires adverse-event protocols.
Fortified rice introduction may face acceptability challenges; micro-dose framing and escalation pathways are recommended.
Documentation of anemia (e.g., low Hb levels) and clinical decisions must be communicated to school staff for follow-up.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: PM POSHAN provides daily hot cooked meals, but the school platform also acts as a delivery site for preventive interventions that sit under the health system (weekly IFA, biannual NDD, and screening campaigns).
document_key_topics: PM POSHAN and school meal programs
Iron and folic acid supplementation in schools
